description: Free GPU provider rotation state for Ouro model training. Quota windows reset weekly (Monday 00:00 UTC). Providers are tried in priority order; exhausted providers are skipped until their window resets.

providers.notes: Best free programmatic option. REST kernel push via /api/v1/kernels/push. Quota refills Monday midnight.
setup_steps: 1. Register at https://www.kaggle.com/account/login?phase=startRegisterTab
2. Verify your phone number (required to enable GPU in notebooks)
3. Go to https://www.kaggle.com/settings/account → API Tokens → 'Generate New Token' → copy the KGAT_... value
4. Set env var (User scope, persistent): [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('KAGGLE_API_TOKEN', 'KGAT_...', 'User')
5. Legacy alternative: Settings → Legacy API Credentials → Create → download kaggle.json → set KAGGLE_USERNAME + KAGGLE_KEY
6. Dispatcher auto-detects: prefers KAGGLE_API_TOKEN (Bearer), falls back to KAGGLE_USERNAME+KAGGLE_KEY (Basic)

providers.notes: No official API. Orchestrator emits a pre-built notebook with checkpoint URI; user opens and clicks Run.
setup_steps: 1. Any Google account works — sign up at https://accounts.google.com/signup if needed
2. Open https://colab.research.google.com
3. When the orchestrator emits a manual_required record, it includes notebookTemplate — copy that code into a new Colab notebook
4. Runtime → Change runtime type → T4 GPU → Save
5. Run All (Ctrl+F9) — session auto-terminates after 8–12 h
6. No env vars needed for Colab; orchestrator posts the checkpoint URI into the notebook code

providers.notes: 4h GPU sessions, up to 4h/day free. Refills daily. Manual launch required. No AWS account or credit card needed.
setup_steps: 1. Request a free account at https://studiolab.sagemaker.aws/signup — no AWS account or credit card required
2. Wait for approval email (typically a few hours to a few days)
3. Click the link in the approval email to set your username and password
4. Sign in at https://studiolab.sagemaker.aws/login
5. Click 'Start runtime' → select GPU (T4, 4h session) → Open project
6. Paste the notebookTemplate from the manual_required record into a new notebook cell and run it
7. Files persist between sessions — only the runtime resets after 4 h

providers.unavailable_reason: Free GPU machines now require a paid PRO subscription. No truly free GPU tier as of 2026.
providers.notes: 8GB VRAM — fits seq=1536 but no headroom. REST API available. Now part of DigitalOcean. FREE GPU REQUIRES PRO SUB — skipped in rotation.
setup_steps: 1. Free GPU now requires PRO subscription (~$8/mo) — skip unless paid
2. If paid: sign up at https://www.paperspace.com/account/signup
3. After login, go to https://console.paperspace.com/account/api
4. Click 'Add' → name your key (e.g. ouro-training) → copy the key
5. Set env var: PAPERSPACE_API_KEY=<your_key> and restore quota_hours_per_week to 20

providers.notes: 22 free GPU credits/month after phone verify. A10 (24GB) available when T4 busy. Manual launch.
setup_steps: 1. Sign up at https://lightning.ai/sign-up — .edu or company email gets instant approval; personal email may wait ~48 h
2. Verify your phone number to unlock 22 free GPU credits/month (1 credit ≈ 1 T4 GPU-hour)
3. Go to account settings → Keys → Programmatic Login — copy LIGHTNING_USER_ID and LIGHTNING_API_KEY
4. Set env vars (User scope, persistent): [System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('LIGHTNING_USER_ID', '<id>', 'User') and same for LIGHTNING_API_KEY
5. Dispatch is now fully automated: orchestrator calls scripts/lightning_dispatch.py dispatch → Studio.start() → switch_machine(T4) → run training → auto-stop on done
6. Studio name is ouro-training by default; override via LIGHTNING_STUDIO_NAME env var
